derivation: Start 45.0 (round-10 score of record per TRAJECTORY.md, with the -0.4 mouse-look human override and -0.1 swim debit applied; the campaign's 45.6/0-of-3 line was stale and has been amended). Two seats this round: delta 45.5, hunter 45.4. Agreement on the credits: redstone slice 2 +0.40-0.45 (doors with held-W walk telemetry, true momentary button on simNow through an 18.3s pause freeze, persistence incl. mid-press re-arm), breadth pack +0.25 (berry loop, mutton-furnace second economy, birch/sandstone/stone-brick with real-path TOOLREQ gating, fingerprints bit-identical), swim-jump reversal +0.10, HUD polish +0.05. Agreement on the headline debit: the probe's world-spawn drift is source-confirmed (game/index.html:941 if(!SV) skips the spiral; :949 captures the loaded position as SPAWN); the hunter widened its blast radius (falsifies the r10 bedgone-parity credit on loaded sessions, fires for every real player via the 15s autosave, re-centers all three probe finders). The hunter also carries a net-new verified bug the delta seat never priced: picked bushes (39) have no load re-arm mirroring line 1543's crop re-arm, so the credited renewable berry loop is intra-session only -- I verified the asymmetry at source (1533/1543 vs 1052). Mouse-look -0.4 stays parked per the standing human-override precedent; synthetic hardening evidence earns nothing. Merge: median of 45.5/45.4 is 45.45; ties break down, and the evidence-heavier seat (hunter, all findings source-verified, none unverified-heavy) holds the lower number with an unpriced real defect -- 45.4. Round delta +0.4, under the +1.0 bar: exit counter advances to 2/3.
exit_recommendation: Exit: the counter hits 2/3 this round, my best-lane projection (+0.9, breadth bonus excluded as ranking-only) is under +1.0 and would complete the trailing rule, and the rounds-10-12 rolling cost projects to ~2.0-2.5M tokens per MPS point -- crossing the 2M soft ceiling -- so run Kevin's zero-token human checkpoint (mouse-look certification with fast-flick and foreground-FPS tests, ~+0.9 potential on the parked -0.4 plus the dark input cluster) and then take the final rating-only panel.

probe.observations: ROUND 11 PROBE: zero page errors across the whole session (2 page instances, ~30 evals, 1 death, 1 save/reload); console clean except the known three.js r160 deprecation; heap 33MB, perf.build avg 3.41ms/chunk (max 80ms first-gen) within 2x of the 2.8ms baseline; pwrCalls 0 across 300 idle frames (edit-triggered-only preserved). THE ROUND-10 PROBE HOLE IS CLOSED: the diagonal-gap failure is gone - a straight lever-3-wire-door run opens/closes the door with synchronous same-eval id reads AND held-W telemetry (blocked at x=110.699 closed, walks to 114.88 powered open), and the button gives a true momentary pulse on both a lamp and a door, with the pause oracle holding a press frozen for 18.3 wall-clock seconds. ONE REAL FIND (fail below): post-reload 'world spawn' is wherever you stood at save time, because the spawn search is gated on if(!SV) (line 943) and const SPAWN (line 949) captures the loaded player position - with no bed, dying after a reload teleports you back to your save point, which quietly deletes corpse-run stakes on any loaded session (my corpse-run death 45 blocks out respawned me AT the redstone build where I'd saved, hp 20, drops still out there). Latent in the load path rather than new this round, but newly observable now that saves+beds+corpse-run compose; cheap fix: capture SPAWN from the fresh-boot search result independent of SV, or persist a ws field. CAVEATS FOR JUDGES: (a) mouse-look CORE stays human-pending per the round-10 override - everything I verified (150-clamp arithmetic exact on both axes via genuine document-dispatched MouseEvents, 40000px spike bounded with zero pitch pin, monotonic recovery, lookLog raw ring, injected path intact) is the synthetic shadow of the fix, not the OS pointer-lock path; the first-mousemove-after-lock drop is untestable without real lock engage. (b) brk() is a creative-style instant break (doBreak drop=true) that BYPASSES the tool gate - my first TOOLREQ read was a false fail artifact; the real hold-to-mine path (mineHold+step) gates correctly (bare hand ~2.2s no drop, wood pick ~0.7s with drop). Future probers: never use brk() for drop-gating questions. (c) Placement rays hit non-solid deco: a grass tuft at (108,32,160) ate my wire placement and the wire landed ON TOP of the tuft (floating wire on non-solid support at (108,33,160)) - the refusal cue fired honestly on the blocked retries, but wire/deco support rules accept a tuft as floor; cosmetic nitpick worth a cleanup line in some future slice. (d) Headless rAF starvation unchanged: HUD FPS 0 in screenshots is the known artifact; everything in this report rides step()/synchronous reads. PLAY-FEEL: the lever-click door-swing with the 'pdoor' thunk, then walking the gap before a button pulse slams it behind you, is the first genuinely Minecraft-feeling redstone moment; the breadth pack reads instantly in screenshots (white birch bark against oak is unmistakable; sandstone/stone-brick/furnace cluster at spawn looks like a build palette now), and the new HUD is pixel-perfect mirror-symmetric at 1600x900 with the drumstick row depleting from the left exactly like MC. Berry bushes make plains foraging a real loop (harvest-39-regrow-38 in 60s); cooked-vs-raw mutton (6 vs 3) gives the furnace a second economy. Breadth verified at ~35 tool calls total.

verified_fail.feature: Post-reload world spawn drifts to the saved player position (no-bed respawn after ?load=1)
verified_fail.evidence: On the ?test=1&load=1 instance with no bed placed, dying by fall at (150.5,~,140.5) and clicking Respawn returned the player to (110.5,32,164.5) - the exact spot where save() was called - NOT world spawn (116.5,29.02,115.5). Root cause read from source: index.html line 943 gates the spawn search on if(!SV) so it never runs on a loaded boot, then line 949 const SPAWN={x:pos.x,...} captures the just-loaded player position as 'world spawn'. Consequence: any save taken in the field turns into a free respawn anchor without crafting a bed, deleting corpse-run stakes on loaded sessions. Fresh-page behavior (round-10 verified world-spawn respawn) is unaffected.

delta.delta_assessment: Start 45.0 (round-10 merge incl. the -0.4 mouse-look human override and -0.1 swim debit). CREDITS: +0.40 redstone slice 2 — the consensus #1 gap (priced 0.3-0.5 by four seats; the 0.5 prices included an inverter that did not ship) cashed in full: powered doors both directions with held-W telemetry (blocked x=110.699, walks to 114.88 open), momentary button as a true 15-power source riding simNow (18.3s pause-freeze oracle), button drives lamp light-registry + hostile-spawn gate, persistence including mid-press button re-arm with no stuck network; family ~2/4 -> ~3/4, still no inverter/logic and propagation stays instant-on-edit. +0.25 breadth pack (unpriced owner directive): berry bush opens the farming family with a verified renewable loop (harvest 39, 60s regrow, +2 food, ~+0.15), mutton raw +3 / furnace-cooked +6 gives smelting a second verified economy (+0.05), birch variant + sandstone/stone-brick with correct TOOLREQ gating on the real mineHold path (+0.05); worldgen fingerprints bit-identical so no determinism debit. +0.10 swim-jump: exact reversal of the round-10 human debit — physics is synthetically observable (unlike pointer-lock), 3/3 bank mounts at y=27.001 with the open-water 3.2/-2.8 clamp regression intact. +0.05 HUD mirror: round 10 absorbed the asymmetry in the tie-break with no numeric debit, so the pixel-perfect 0.0px fix earns only fresh polish credit. +0.00 mouse-look core: stays FAIL per the human-override precedent; the synthetic hardening (bounded 40000px spike, monotonic recovery, lookLog) is evidence the fix is plausible, not certified; the -0.4 stays parked. DEBITS: -0.25 post-reload world-spawn drift, a new verified fail I source-confirmed (line 941 if(!SV) skips the spawn search on loaded boots; line 949 const SPAWN captures the loaded player position; line 935 shows only a bed overrides it) — any no-bed death after ?load=1 respawns at the save point, deleting corpse-run stakes (a credited 65-band composition) on every loaded session; latent not a lane regression, and save/load otherwise held bit-identical, so it prices below the mouse-look override. -0.05 placement rays accept a non-solid grass tuft as support, leaving verified floating wire. Net +0.50 -> 45.5. Tie-break check: arithmetic lands exactly on 45.5, no rounding generosity applied; zero probe regressions, heap down 47->33MB, build 3.41ms within 2x baseline, pwrCalls 0 idle. Delta +0.5 is under the +1.0 exit bar (counter would move to 2/3).

top_gaps.title: Human checkpoint: certify real mouse-look + sweep the ten-round dark input cluster
top_gaps.detail: The single largest item on the board costs zero tokens. A 10-minute hands-on session certifies the c8cb1fa pointer-lock fix (lifts the parked -0.4: synthetic evidence shows exact 150-clamp arithmetic, bounded spikes, monotonic recovery, but only real lock engage can test the first-mousemove-after-lock drop) AND converts the dark checklist mass no probe can reach: pointer lock with re-lock, A/D strafe after turning, ceiling collision, right-click context menu suppression, wall sliding, diagonal speed, pillar jumping, interaction-through-walls, foreground 60fps. Player seat priced the full headed pass at +1.1; harsh discount because some dark items may genuinely fail when first tried by hand.

top_gaps.title: Fix post-reload world-spawn drift (restore this round's -0.25)
top_gaps.detail: index.html line 941 gates the spawn search on if(!SV) and line 949 captures the just-loaded player position as SPAWN, so every loaded session gets a free respawn anchor at the save point with no bed. Cheap fix per the probe: persist a ws field in the v:1 save (optional field, old saves keep current behavior or recompute) or run the deterministic spawn search regardless of SV. Must re-verify: fresh-boot spawn (116.5,29.02,115.5) unchanged, loaded-boot no-bed death returns to world spawn, bed spawn still takes precedence, bedgone fallback intact, corpse-run stakes restored on a loaded session.

top_gaps.title: Hamlet inhabitant + loot chest (fifth round stalled at ~2.3/3)
top_gaps.detail: Villages have now stalled five rounds while redstone, beds, bows, and breadth all landed around them. A villager-flavored passive on the proven MOBDEF substrate leashed to the (488,440) hamlet center, plus a take-only chest reusing the furnace slot machinery with 2-3 seeded items, converts the lit night destination into a payoff. Keep writes inside the single-chunk footprint; re-verify the hamlet fingerprint, the 0.090 interior light value, and the 2-torch registration bit-identical. Priced 0.4-0.45 by three seats in round 10 and unaddressed.

top_gaps.title: Redstone slice 3: redstone-torch inverter (the family's first logic)
top_gaps.detail: Doors + button moved the family to ~3/4 but there is still no NOT gate, so no logic, no oscillators, no locks - the systems and perf seats' 0.5 prices were inverter-inclusive and only the doors+button fraction cashed. A redstone torch (crafts from coal dust + stick, powers adjacent wire at 15, turns OFF when its support block is powered) rides the existing edit-triggered BFS and block-id state model, so v:1 saves and the pause oracle carry over. Verify: torch-on-wall powers a wire run, lever powering the support block kills it, two-torch chain restores signal, persistence through reload.

top_gaps.title: Farming proper: tilled soil + planted crop with growth stages
top_gaps.detail: The berry bush opened the farming family (~0.5/2) as foraging; the family's core is player agriculture. A hoe interaction (or reuse an existing tool) tilling grass/dirt, seeds from grass tufts, and a wheat-style crop with 2-3 visible growth stages on a gt timer feeding a bread recipe converts most of the remaining weight. State in block ids like everything else so persistence is free; verify growth stages survive save/reload and the determinism fingerprints hold.

top_gaps.title: Chunk data eviction using the proven editB replay (eleventh-round debt)
top_gaps.detail: Zero chunks.delete for an eleventh round: every visited chunk retains its ~16KB Uint8Array forever. Regeneration is deterministic through four consecutive content drops and editB replay already restores edits on regen; evict Uint8Arrays beyond ~3x render distance mirroring dropMesh's RD+2 test. Must verify the hamletPlaced torch re-scan AND the lit-lamp re-registration path restore torchR on regen, then re-run sinkhole + hamlet + circuit fingerprints after a far round trip. Heap is healthy now (33MB) so this is principle debt, but corpse runs and the growing build palette keep raising the cost of never paying it.

top_gaps.title: Open the gen-placed hamlet crafting table live + cactus brush (pure probe, zero code)
top_gaps.detail: Eleven rounds in, the hamlet's 3x3 table block (b===17 interact branch) has never been right-clicked in play - every recipe including this round's 13-recipe shadow matrix resolved via matchGrid evals. One eval session at the (488,440) hut cashes the crafting family's last ~0.3 weight; brushing a cactus for contact damage closes another tenth-round-owed probe item. Cheapest verified MPS on the board after the human checkpoint.

top_gaps.title: Placement support hygiene: reject non-solid deco as support + zero-item refusal cue
top_gaps.detail: Two verified paper cuts from this round's probe: (a) placement rays treat a grass tuft as floor, leaving floating wire on non-solid support at (108,33,160) - make deco transparent to support checks or auto-pop the deco like MC; (b) place() with zero items of the selected type silently no-ops where every other refusal has an audible cue. Both are small, contained edits to the place path; verify the refuse sfx ledger and the no-floater sweep afterward.

delta.summary: Round 11 nets +0.5 to MPS 45.5. The headline is clean execution against the priced board: redstone slice 2 cashed exactly as four seats priced it (+0.4) - powered doors with walk telemetry, a true momentary button riding simNow through an 18.3s pause freeze, persistence including mid-press re-arm - closing the round-10 probe hole, and the unpriced owner breadth directive landed without breaking a single determinism fingerprint (+0.25: berry-bush renewable food loop opens farming, cooked mutton gives the furnace a second economy, birch/sandstone/stone-brick read instantly in screenshots). Two of the three human-filed findings are verifiably fixed and earn their reversals (+0.1 swim bank-mount, +0.05 HUD mirror), but the big one stays parked: the mouse-look core item remains FAIL and the -0.4 stays withheld until human hands certify the real pointer-lock path - the synthetic hardening evidence is encouraging and worth exactly nothing under the override precedent. Against that, the probe's one real find debits -0.25: a source-confirmed load-path bug (lines 941/949) makes any save point a free no-bed respawn anchor on loaded sessions, quietly deleting the corpse-run stakes the build earned in round 10; plus -0.05 for verified floating wire on a grass tuft. Zero regressions, zero errors, heap down to 33MB. The +0.5 delta sits under the +1.0 exit bar. The next round's largest items cost almost no tokens at the current 1.74M/point burn: a 10-minute human session (certify mouse-look, sweep the ten-round dark input cluster, ~+0.9 potential) and a one-line spawn fix (+0.25) together dwarf any code lane on the board.

regressions.title: CONFIRMED + WIDER BLAST RADIUS: post-reload 'world spawn' is the save point (probe's fail is real, and it also breaks the round-10 'bedgone' parity credit and re-centers every probe finder)
regressions.evidence: Code-verified: game/index.html:941 gates the spawn spiral on if(!SV), then line 949 `const SPAWN={x:pos.x,y:pos.y,z:pos.z}` captures the just-loaded player position. Beyond the probe's filing, the blast radius is larger than reported: (a) the respawn handler (line 1795-1797) uses SPAWN for BOTH the no-bed path AND the bed-destroyed 'bedgone' fallback, so round 10's credited 'bed destroyed -> falls back to world spawn (MC parity)' is false on any loaded session - bedgone falls back to wherever you saved; (b) since real (non-TEST) play autosaves every 15s and on tab-hide (lines 939-940), EVERY real-player reload silently converts their last position into a free respawn anchor, devaluing beds permanently, not just in probe scenarios; (c) findStructures (1882), findCaveMouth (1934), and findOverhang (1954) all center on SPAWN, so on ?load=1 instances every finder re-centers on the save point - future probers comparing 'bit-identical fingerprints' on loaded pages against fresh-boot baselines will get false mismatches. Fix is the probe's: capture SPAWN from the fresh-boot search independent of SV, or persist a ws field.

regressions.title: NEW, unfiled by the probe: harvested berry bushes are permanently barren after save/reload - crops re-arm on load, bushes don't
regressions.evidence: game/index.html:1543 re-arms saved crops into the farm map (`if(ev===19||ev===20)farm.set(ek,gt+15)`) and line 1052 re-arms saved pressed buttons (ev===43) - but there is no ev===39 re-arm into the transient bushes queue (line 1533, 'NEVER saved'). updFarm (1540-1542) only regrows queued entries, so a bush saved in its picked state (39) never returns to 38; breaking the 39 yields nothing (DROPS deliberately omits 38/39, line 435) and deletes the bush forever. The round-11 probe verified 'harvested bush 39 persisted' through reload and stopped there - it never tested regrow-after-reload, so this asymmetry slipped past. The header (line 140-141) flags 'a reload may legitimately show a harvested bush still at 39' but does NOT flag that it stays 39 forever, which contradicts the renewable-foraging loop the probe credited ('berry bushes make plains foraging a real loop'). One-line fix mirroring 1543: re-arm ev===39 into bushes at load.

regressions.title: Documented but never play-checked: any edit on an UNPOWERED network slams manually-opened doors shut
regressions.evidence: updatePower's door branch (game/index.html:724-732) rewrites the pair toward dp on EVERY network update, including dp===0: a free-standing door manually opened (33/34), then a single wire placed adjacent, triggers setB->updatePower with an empty pow map -> (dp>0)!==op -> halves rewritten closed with a 'pdoor' thunk. The header flags 'power state WINS whenever a power-network update touches a door' (lines 63-66), so this is honest substitution, not a hidden bug - but the concrete consequence (laying redstone dust NEXT TO your propped-open door shuts it on you, repeatedly, while you build the circuit) has never been exercised by any probe, and it is exactly the kind of friction the human checkpoint should feel before the family gets priced toward 3/4. No probe round has placed a wire adjacent to a manually-opened door on a sourceless network.

overcredits.title: Save/load family carried at 'weight 2, full and strengthened' (round-10 systems/worldgen seats) while loaded-session respawn semantics are wrong
overcredits.evidence: Round-10 panel credits 'World save/load (weight 2, full)' and the worldgen seat calls it 'strongest round yet'. The round-11 verified fail shows the load path corrupts the world-spawn anchor: dying after any reload without a bed teleports you to the save point (hp 20, drops abandoned), deleting corpse-run stakes and making beds redundant on every loaded session. A weight-2-FULL family whose post-load death semantics diverge from both MC and the build's own fresh-boot behavior should be docked (~0.2-0.3 of the family) until fixed; the persistence of block ids/gt/inventory is real, but 'full' overstates a family with a live defect in its core composition with death/respawn.

overcredits.title: Steady-FPS core item is on its ELEVENTH round of rAF-starvation exoneration with zero foreground measurement
overcredits.evidence: Round-11 probe again reports 'HUD FPS 0 in screenshots is the known artifact; everything in this report rides step()/synchronous reads' and substitutes builder-side perf.build (3.41ms/chunk, within 2x of baseline). The rendering seat's round-10 verdict already conceded 'interactive foreground confirmation still owed'. The exoneration was reasonable when filed; carrying a CORE item on it for 11 consecutive rounds while the scene has since gained lamp light-pool remeshes, doors, buttons, birch forests, and bushes is evidence rot. The human checkpoint (already mandated for mouse-look) should bank or break this item in the same session - sprint/spin through the redstone causeway at the heaviest light-pool scene.

overcredits.title: 'Bit-identical tree positions' is construction-proven, not measured - and the one fingerprint that actually scans trees (the hamlet) was NOT re-read in round 11
overcredits.evidence: The birch claim rests on the code argument (game/index.html:595-606: bch swaps TK/LF ids inside oak's exact loops - which I verify reads correctly), but the round-11 fingerprints that passed (spawn coords, findCaveMouth sites, gt) sample ci.tree only at sparse spiral/site points and do not fingerprint tree layout. The (488,440) hamlet census - whose gate scans a 13x13 for trees (line 653) and whose byType counts WOULD catch a tree-pass regression - went unverified this round for the first time since round 8, in exactly the round a lane touched the tree pass. Risk is low (the hash argument is airtight on my read, and bushes are gated out of %37/%89 chunks at line 626), but the panel should not carry 'villages ~2.3/3' plus 'bit-identical worldgen' on a round where the structures fingerprint was skipped; one findStructures eval next round closes it.

overcredits.title: Lane-1 synthetic mouse-look evidence has an uncharacterized real-input edge the human checkpoint must cover: the 150px/event clamp is also a turn-rate ceiling
overcredits.evidence: Under real pointer lock Chromium coalesces raw input into ~one mousemove per rendered frame, so one event carries a full frame's delta; the clamp (game/index.html:1048) truncates any |delta|>150px. At 60fps the ceiling is 150*0.0022*60 = ~19.8 rad/s (~1130 deg/s, probably imperceptible), but at 30fps it halves to ~565 deg/s - within the range of an ordinary fast 180-degree flick, which would feel like negative mouse acceleration. The probe's 40000px spike test proves bounding works AND proves genuine large deltas get truncated; the synthetic suite cannot distinguish 'spike suppressed' from 'flick eaten'. Kevin's hands-on session should include fast flicks, ideally while the tab is under load. This does not change any credit (the core item is already FAIL/human-pending) but the checkpoint script should test it explicitly or the certification will be hollow on exactly the failure mode that matters.

overcredits.title: brk()-based drop evidence from earlier rounds is retroactively tainted for tool-gating claims
overcredits.evidence: Round-11 probe caveat (b) is correct and code-confirmed: __vox.brk routes tryBreak -> doBreak(h,true) (game/index.html:1844, 1117-1119), hard-coding drop=true and bypassing the TOOLREQ gate that the real mineHold path applies (line 1199-1203). Any prior-round credit that inferred tier-gated drops from brk() calls is unsound evidence; the round-6 mining conversion used controlled hold-to-mine rhythm and round-11 re-proved the real path on stone bricks (bare ~2.2s no drop / wood pick ~0.7s with drop), so CURRENT credit stands - but the panel ledger should annotate brk() as gate-bypassing so no future seat cites it for TOOLREQ claims.

hunter.summary: Hunted the three round-10 lanes' full blast radius in code (input handler, vitals CSS, tree pass/CI deco chain, atlas 128->256 retile, power BFS + button timers, and every shared table: BL/NSOLID/NAMES/DROPS/HARD/TOOLREQ/RECIPES/ITILE/FOOD/SMELT/FUEL). The lanes are clean against previously verified items: the birch reskin and bush placement provably cannot move existing block ids (bush branch is genuinely last in the deco chain, placement is air-only-above-intact-caps, structure-candidate chunks gated out), the recipe matrix has no new shadowing (button {3:1} vs bricks {3:4} vs furnace {3:5} resolve correctly under most-specific-wins, key-set count protects the lever), tuv/avg handle the taller atlas correctly, and the door/button power paths handle orphan halves, FIFO eviction, mid-break pops, and re-arm-on-load coherently. I found no broken previously-verified item. Real findings: (1) the probe's SPAWN-drift fail is confirmed at index.html:941/949 and is WORSE than filed - it also falsifies the round-10 'bedgone -> world spawn' parity credit on loaded sessions, fires for every real player via the 15s autosave, and re-centers all three probe finders post-load; (2) net-new: reloaded picked bushes (39) never regrow because the load re-arm at line 1543 covers crops and line 1052 covers buttons but nothing re-queues 39 - permanent barren bushes, one-line fix; (3) the documented power-wins substitution means placing one wire next to a manually-opened door slams it shut even on a sourceless network - header-flagged but never play-checked. Overcredit watch: save/load family should be docked from 'full' until the spawn anchor is fixed; steady-FPS core rides the rAF exoneration an 11th round; the hamlet fingerprint (the only one that scans trees) was skipped in exactly the round the tree pass changed; and the 150px clamp doubles as a turn-rate ceiling at low fps that the human checkpoint must explicitly flick-test before certifying mouse-look. Score: 45.0 baseline + ~0.45 for the verified redstone slice 2 (the round-10 probe hole is genuinely closed, with pause-oracle and persistence coverage) + ~0.25 for the cleanly verified breadth pack (berry loop, mutton-furnace economy, TOOLREQ-correct bricks) - ~0.15 for the spawn-drift defect and its retroactive bedgone falsification, ties broken down: 45.4. Mouse-look core stays FAIL pending the human checkpoint per the standing override.

lanes.name: Lane A — Correctness fix pack: world-spawn anchor, bush re-arm, placement support hygiene
lanes.goal: Restore the round-11 debits (-0.25 spawn drift, -0.05 floating wire) and fix the hunter's net-new bush-regrow bug before they compound; all low-risk, contained edits to the load and place paths of game/index.html.
lanes.spec: (1) World spawn: at lines 941-949, run the deterministic spawn spiral UNCONDITIONALLY into a local `best`; assign pos from it only when !SV (loaded boots must not move the player); derive `const SPAWN = best ? {x:best[0]+.5, y:best[1]+1.02, z:best[2]+.5} : {x:pos.x,y:pos.y,z:pos.z}`. No save-schema change; old v:1 saves load identically. This also re-anchors findStructures/findCaveMouth/findOverhang (lines 1882/1934/1954), which center on SPAWN. (2) Bush re-arm: beside line 1543's crop re-arm, add `for(const[ek,ev]of edits) if(ev===39){const p=ek.split(','); if(bushes.length<256) bushes.push({x:+p[0],y:+p[1],z:+p[2],t:gt});}` honoring the FIFO cap; update the line-1533 comment and the header note near lines 140-141 (a reloaded picked bush now regrows). (3) Placement hygiene in tryPlace (line 1167): when the ray hit h.b is a non-solid deco cross-plane (5/13/14/38/39), refuse it as a support face with sfx + logS('refuse') (or re-cast through it); wire (27) placement additionally requires SOLID(getB(c.x,c.y-1,c.z)); add logS('refuse') to the zero-item branch at line 1185 so the refusal is ledger-visible.
lanes.acceptance: Fresh ?test=1 boot: spawn exactly (116.5,29.02,115.5); sinkhole + hamlet (findStructures census at 488,440 -- skipped in r11, must be re-read) + circuit fingerprints bit-identical. On ?test=1&load=1 with a save taken far afield and NO bed: die via __vox, respawn returns to (116.5,29.02,115.5), not the save point; with a bed the bed wins; break the bed and 'bedgone' logs with respawn at true world spawn. Save a picked bush (39), reload, __vox.step gt forward >=60s: block returns to 38 and re-harvest yields berries. Wire placement attempt onto a grass tuft (the (108,33,160) repro): 'refuse' appears in __vox.sfx, no floating wire in a sweep; zero-item place also logs 'refuse'. Build time within 2x baseline, pwrCalls 0 idle.

lanes.name: Lane B — Hamlet inhabitant + take-only loot chest (villages to 3/3)
lanes.goal: Convert the lit night destination into a payoff after five stalled rounds: a villager-flavored passive leashed to the (488,440) hamlet plus a seeded take-only chest, folding in the two zero-code probe items (gen-placed crafting table live + cactus brush).
lanes.spec: New block 44 'Chest' (NAMES line 434, BL line 431) with atlas tiles 72-73 (8x16 atlas, rows 9+; tiles 0-71 untouched). Placed once by the hamlet generator (hamletPlaced block, lines 649-675) on a hut floor cell INSIDE the existing single-chunk footprint. Contents in a `chests` Map keyed 'x,y,z' mirroring furnaces (line 474), lazily seeded deterministically from h3f of the chest coords (e.g. 2 bread 109, 3 torch 22, 1 iron ingot 104); persisted as optional save field `chs` exactly like `furn` at lines 931/937 so old saves are unaffected. Interact: take-only UI reusing the furnace slot machinery, dispatched from tryPlace beside the b===17/23 branches (lines 1168-1169) and from the __vox.interact b-dispatch (line ~1978); clicking a slot addItem()s the stack, no deposit path. Villager: new MOBDEF entry (line 1274) -- passive, robe-colored parts reusing the pig/sheep wander logic, hp ~10; spawned cap-aware (MOBMAX) when the hamlet chunk gens/regens, leashed within ~10 blocks of hamlet center; transient like all mobs. IDs/tiles disjoint from Lane C (which owns 45/46 and tiles 74-75).
lanes.acceptance: findStructures census at (488,440): byType diff is exactly the one chest cell (replacing interior air); interior light value 0.090 and the 2-torch torchR registration unchanged; all other fingerprints bit-identical. Right-click the gen-placed table (b===17) at the hamlet LIVE -- first time in 11 rounds -- and complete one craft through the real UI; brush a cactus for contact damage en route (closes both owed probe items, ~+0.15). Chest opens via real right-click AND __vox.interact; take all three stacks; reload: taken items stay taken via the chs field; verify no deposit path exists. Villager stays within the 10-block leash across a 120s __vox.step soak, survives a chunk regen round trip, and hostile spawn-gating inside the lit hamlet is unchanged.

lanes.name: Lane C — Redstone slice 3: redstone-torch inverter (the family's first logic)
lanes.goal: Add the NOT gate the systems/perf seats' round-10 prices assumed (only the doors+button fraction cashed): a redstone torch that sources 15, dies when its support block is powered, and chains into real logic -- moving redstone toward 4/4 on the proven edit-triggered BFS.
lanes.spec: New blocks 45 'Redstone Torch' (lit) / 46 'Redstone Torch (off)' as lever-style cross-plane nubs (BL line 431 pattern), atlas tiles 74-75. Floor-mounted only, support = block directly below -- flagged pragmatic substitution vs MC wall mounting. Recipe {in:{27:1,100:1},out:45,n:1} (1 redstone-wire item + 1 stick); key-set {27,100} is unique -- run the non-shadowing matrix check against lever {3,100}, torch {100,10}, bow {100,24} per the RECIPES comments (lines 456-472). Power model in updatePower: a lit torch is a 15-power source like lever-on/button-pressed; after the BFS computes pow, evaluate each torch in the touched network -- support (x,y-1,z) counts as powered if any cell adjacent to it carries pow>0 or is an active source OTHER than the torch itself; powered support flips 45->46 (setB + touched + logS('rtorch')), unpowered flips 46->45; the setB re-fires updatePower (instant-on-edit, consistent with the family) under a per-cascade flip budget (~8) so a paradox loop (torch feeding its own support) settles or freezes instead of hanging -- document the no-tick-scheduler budget as a flagged substitution in the header. Mining drops the torch item (DROPS); persistence is free via block ids in edits; confirm load-time recompute restores correct on/off without a stuck network (mirror the button re-arm pattern at line 1052 if needed).
lanes.acceptance: Torch on a block + wire run to a lamp: lamp lights via the light registry, pow 15 at the torch decaying along the wire. Lever -> wire -> the torch's support block: lever ON kills the torch (45->46, lamp dark), lever OFF restores it, 'rtorch' entries visible in __vox.sfx. Two-torch chain (NOT of NOT): lever ON yields output ON, lever OFF yields output OFF. Save mid-state and reload: torch states + circuit behavior identical; __vox.pause() freeze does not corrupt state; a torch wired to its own support settles within the flip budget with no frame hang; perf.build within 2x baseline, pwrCalls 0 at idle; sinkhole/hamlet/circuit fingerprints bit-identical.

built.lane: Lane A — Correctness fix pack: world-spawn anchor, bush re-arm, placement support hygiene
built.changes_summary: Three contained edits to game/index.html. (1) World-spawn anchor (was lines 941-949): the deterministic spawn spiral now runs UNCONDITIONALLY into a local `best` (pure CI/cave/sinkR reads, no mutation); pos is assigned from it only when !SV, and `const SPAWN = best ? {x:best[0]+.5,y:best[1]+1.02,z:best[2]+.5} : {x:pos.x,y:pos.y,z:pos.z}`. Loaded boots keep the saved position but SPAWN (and findStructures/findCaveMouth/findOverhang plus the no-bed respawn) no longer drifts to the save point. No save-schema change. (2) Bush re-arm: added beside the line-1543 crop re-arm a loop over edits that pushes every ev===39 into the bushes queue with t=gt, honoring the 256 FIFO cap; updated the bushes-declaration comment and the round-10 header note (a reloaded picked bush now regrows). (3) Placement hygiene in tryPlace: new early refusal when the ray hit h.b is a non-solid deco cross-plane (5/13/14/38/39) with sfx + logS('refuse'); wire (27) placement now additionally requires SOLID(getB(c.x,c.y-1,c.z)) (refuse cue + ledger entry otherwise); the zero-item branch now logs 'refuse' too.
built.notes: Static: file ends with </html>; node --check on the extracted 139,903-char inline script passes. Runtime-verified in the live preview (port 8911): fresh ?test=1 spawns exactly (116.5,29.02,115.5) with SPAWN matching; findStructures(512) returns 21 entries including the hamlet at (488,31,440); findCaveMouth(160) productive (4 mouths, nearest 129,32,148). Loaded boot with a far-afield no-bed save (516.5,40,515.5): player boots at the save point, die+respawn returns to (116.5,29.02,115.5); bed placed+used wins respawn (118.5,28.02,108.5); bed broken logs 'bedgone' and respawns at true world spawn. Bush: natural 38 at (93,28,134) harvested to 39, saved, reloaded -> bushes queue re-armed; gt+61 + farm tick regrew it to 38 and re-harvest dropped 2x berries (118). Placement: wire onto flower(13)/tuft(14) logs 'refuse' with zero wires in a volume sweep; wire onto a side face with air below logs 'refuse' (no floating wire); wire on solid ground still places; stone placement unaffected; zero-item place logs 'refuse'. pwrCalls 0 idle, __vox.errors 0, no console errors, frame avg 0.1ms. Spec deviation note: the (108,33,160) repro coordinate is air in the current world; the deco at that column is a flower (13) at y=32 (tufts nearby at 106,31,160) - both were tested and refuse correctly. Test localStorage saves were cleared and the preview reset to a pristine ?test=1 boot.

built.lane: Lane B — Hamlet inhabitant + take-only loot chest (villages to 3/3)
built.changes_summary: Block 44 'Chest' added (BL t:[72,72,73], NAMES, HARD .8; deliberately ABSENT from DROPS so a placeable chest can't reseed loot — breaking spills contents via a seed-then-spill doBreak branch instead). New atlas tiles 72/73 on row 9 (plank lid + latch front); tiles 0-71 untouched. Hamlet generator places the chest once at hut A's interior floor corner (local 4,4 = world 484,F+1,436), chosen off both torch sight lines — verified by bit-diffing the generated chunk with/without the chest line: byType diff is exactly the one air→chest cell, interior light probe reads 0.0903 before AND after, torchR keeps exactly its 2 entries. Contents live in a `chests` Map keyed 'x,y,z' mirroring furnaces, lazily seeded from h3i of the cell (2-3 bread/3-4 torch/1-2 ingot; seed at this hamlet: 3/4/1), persisted as optional save field `chs` beside `furn` (v:1 untouched; emptied entries persist as nulls so taken stays taken — verified through a real save/reload). Take-only UI reuses the islot machinery: 3-slot CHEST panel with no arrow/output slot and no deposit path (slotClickT only ever addItem()s out; deposit attempt with a held stack verified inert); dispatched from tryPlace beside the b===17/23 branches and from __vox.interact. Villager: new passive MOBDEF (robe-brown head/nose/robe/folded-arms/legs, hp 10, spd 1.1) on the pig/sheep wander+flee brain (flee on hit included), spawned cap-aware via spawnMob whenever a hamlet chunk's mesh builds (new `hamlets` anchor map written by genChunk, spawnVillager hook in buildChunk, deduped per anchor) and leashed to the hamlet center — past 10 blocks it steers home every frame. Live-verified: 120s step soak max excursion 9.89 blocks; cull-and-return chunk-regen round trip re-seats it at the anchor; remesh dedupe holds; lit-path hostile gating unchanged ('light' at midnight); a real-UI craft completed at the gen-placed hamlet table (log→4 planks via DOM events). Probes added: __vox.chestInfo (map-only peek), __vox.chestTake, mem().chests.
built.notes: Verification went beyond static: (1) node --check on the extracted script passes, file ends with </html>; (2) a Node harness built from the extracted pure worldgen chain (h2i..genChunk..lightAtW) proved the one-cell census diff bit-exactly and the 0.090/2-torch fingerprints; (3) full live run in the preview browser (?test=1, three.js loaded, 0 __vox.errors) covered chest open via both real right-click dispatch and interact, real-DOM-click takes, deposit-attempt rejection, chs persistence across a reload, villager spawn/leash soak/regen round trip/dedupe, spawn-gating, and a real-UI table craft. Caveats for the rater: the preview tab is rAF-throttled (hidden), so mesh builds were driven by calling updChunks() directly — identical code path, but a human visual pass at 60fps wasn't possible from here; the hands-on cactus-brush and live table-craft acceptance items are player-feel checks (the cactus damage code is untouched and the table path is proven working). The 0.090 light probe cell was reverse-engineered as hut A interior (485.5,F+1.5,438.5); if the rater's probe uses a different cell, the bit-identical-except-one-cell diff still bounds any drift to occlusion paths through (484,F+1,436), which I verified intersects no torch ray to any hut A/B interior or path cell. localStorage on the test origin was left clean (no pre-existing save existed; test saves removed). The villager spawns even under ?test=1 without setSpawning (it is deterministic, unlike spawnTick's random spawns) — flagged in the header comment; boot-time probes are unaffected since no hamlet chunk meshes within RD of spawn on seed 1337.

built.lane: Lane C — Redstone slice 3: redstone-torch inverter
built.changes_summary: Implemented the redstone-torch inverter in game/index.html (single file, ~40 lines of code + docs). (1) New blocks 45 'Redstone Torch' (lit) / 46 'Redstone Torch (off)' as lever-style cross-plane nubs, atlas tiles 74/75 (row 9 beside lane B's chest tiles; tiles 0-73 keep exact texels); BL/NSOLID/NAMES/HARD entries mirror torch 22; DROPS normalizes 46->45; lit torch renders self-lit but is deliberately NOT in the LIT light registry (logic element, not light source). (2) Recipe {in:{27:1,100:1},out:45,n:1} — key-set {27,100} verified unique against the full matrix (lever {3,100}, torch {100,10}, bow {100,24}, picks/swords) both statically and live via matchGrid probes; fixes the slice-2 {stick+coal} collision that got the inverter dropped. (3) Power model in updatePower: 45 joins PFAM and the 15-emitter set beside ON-lever/pressed-button; NEW through-support BFS connectivity (a torch seeds its support's 6 neighbors; any cell seeds torches atop adjacent solids) — without this, lever->wire->support chains could never reach the torch (the torch is 2 cells from the wire); after the pow flood each torch in the network is evaluated: support counts powered when any of ITS neighbors except the torch's own cell carries pow>0; flips are collected against the same pow snapshot the rewrite uses and applied AFTER the inPwr guard releases, so each flip's setB re-fires updatePower (instant-on-edit) under a per-cascade budget of 8 shared down the recursion via a depth counter (resets only at top-level edits; exhaustion logs 'rtfreeze' and freezes instead of hanging). (4) Floor-mounted-only placement gate (wire-style solid-below check, flagged substitution vs MC wall mounting); torches pop off broken floors with the 45 drop and depower their circuit. (5) Persistence free via block ids: cascades complete synchronously inside one updatePower call so saves can never catch a half-settled network — verified by save/reload round-trip (no load-time re-arm needed). LIVE-VERIFIED in the browser under ?test=1: torch+wire+lamp lights the lamp into the light registry (wire 28 at pow 14, lamp 30, torch-light 0.876 adjacent); lever->wire->support kills/restores the torch (45<->46, lamp dark/lit, 'rtorch' in __vox.sfx); NOT-of-NOT chain gives lever ON -> output ON, lever OFF -> output OFF (2-flip cascade settles); placing a torch onto a powered support is born 46; save mid-state (lever ON, torch dark) + reload restores bit-identical ids with pwrCalls 0 and identical behavior; __vox.pause() toggles stay consistent; pwrCalls flat across 3 idle sim-seconds; recipe matrix probes confirm zero shadowing; mining drops item 45; zero __vox.errors throughout.
built.notes: Static: file ends </html>; inline script extracted and node --check clean after all edits. Behavioral verification ran live against the preview server (port 8911) via __vox under ?test=1 since the change is data-level and synchronous — full acceptance matrix above passed with errors=0. Two flagged items for the evidence ledger: (a) the spec's literal paradox (torch wired to its own support) is geometrically unconstructible in this floor-mounted model — wires cannot stack (each needs a solid floor) so power planes never descend, and every torch hop ascends one plane; the 8-flip budget is therefore a code-verified safety net ('rtfreeze' path) rather than a reachable state, and its shared-budget mechanics were exercised by the live multi-flip cascades. Documented in the header as the no-tick-scheduler substitution. (b) perf: buildChunk's only delta is one ||b===45 comparison in the cross-plane branch and idle cost is provably zero (pwrCalls flat); the preview tab was rAF-throttled (background) so a clean 2x-baseline frame comparison wasn't measurable there, but no per-frame code path was touched — chunk builds measured 18ms avg/53ms max during the session vs 67ms boot build, well inside normal band. Fingerprints (sinkhole/hamlet/circuit) hold structurally: zero worldgen/deco/structure code touched; ids 45/46 are player-placed only; tiles 74/75 occupy previously-blank atlas space; fresh-boot spawn confirmed (116.5, 29.02, 115.5) on seed 1337 this session. NOT chains deeper than 8 flips freeze mid-cascade and settle on the next edit (documented). localStorage save residue cleared via clearSave after testing. A >8-inverter chain freeze demo and a wall-mounted-torch follow-up are natural next-lane candidates.
